- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "obvious-hutch", "age": "55-64", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "White", "demographic_group": "European", "current_employment": "Employed", "education_level": "Doctorate degree", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Enjoys bird watching", "Active", "Democrat"]}}

logs.name: massive-acquisition
logs.text:
>Only a very small fraction...

There’s a very good reason that statistical arguments don’t hold up in court. They’re totally invalid as stand-alone arguments because they don’t *actually* demonstrate anything. You can’t apply it in an individual case because reality isn’t a big dice game where you can say, “Okay, she made a rape accusation, and 99.5% of them are true. Let’s roll the 200-sided die to decide this one!”

The fact that most assault accusations are true (which in itself is questionable - this isn’t exactly an easy thing to confidently figure out statistically-speaking) I’m sure was very comforting to all the people who’ve had to be exonerated years later because of a false accusation, or those people *still in jail* because we haven’t yet figured out they’re innocent. 

If you were a prosecutor in those cases, would you argue that only a small minority of accusations are lies, and that that should be another reason to convict? What if, 20, 30, 40 years later, the defendant is released because of backlogged DNA evidence that exonerates him - would you stand by that argument? If it were me, I would feel very guilty. I’ve done nothing illegal, but I’d feel as though I’d committed prosecutorial misconduct. 

The point is that that argument doesn’t actually have any bearing on the likelihood of any particular case being true or false. It’s just an average, really. Even if your average bowling score is 260, there’s still nothing stopping you from throwing eleven gutter-balls. It’s essentially a red herring argument that *pretends* to increase the likelihood of one outcome without actually bringing anything substantive to the table. 

I mean, *statistically*, I, as a black man, am much more likely to be killed by another black man in this country. So as a prosecutor, would you look at the jury and say “Statistically, it’s much more likely that the defendant committed this murder because he is black, and that makes it *more likely* that he’s guilty than that suspect #2 is guilty.” 

I guess you wouldn’t, but from a statistical standpoint, that argument is just as valid as the one you made. 

And hell, if I continue to play devil’s advocate, that might make it even more likely; Ford’s a smart social scientist who undoubtedly *knows* this statistic that you’ve given, and she understands psychology, so she knows that statistical arguments can have a lot of power, and that will play into her hand. 

Again, I don’t know anything about Ford personally, so this isn’t an accusation. But from where I stand, there’s nothing to tip the scales of probability in either direction absent actual substantial evidence. That’s why I think we should focus on that kind of evidence. 

>straw-woman

No, because I’m not actually attributing this to her. As I said, it’s an alternative explanation with nothing inherently wrong with it. And by the way, I wrote a second paragraph that had *another* alternative explanation that you didn’t address. It’s not like my scenario hinges on her being a crazy hardcore liberal. She could just be someone who believes that the ends justify the means, I suppose. But again, I don’t know, and I’m not saying that *any* of this is actually true. But we also don’t know that *your* explanation is true.

logs.name: direct-upstream
logs.text:
> Why do you think taking a real assault and changing the culprit is implausible?

Only a very small fraction of people make false accusations of assault.  The likelihood that anyone  *who knows* you (or me, or Kavanaugh) would do this, probably.  Then only a fraction of people were assaulted, so the potential pool of people to commit this swap-eroo is tiny. 

You need a certain improbable level of crazy to concoct an assault, then you need to get get *really really improbable* and have this improbably crazy accuser already have been assaulted by somebody perfectly fitting K's description.  

Improbable is approximately the same as implausible.  The simple and more probable explanation is that she's telling the truth.  

> If someone believes hard enough that conservatives are evil and do not deserve power, 

You're constructing a straw-woman here.

logs.name: potential-potentiality
logs.text: "@massive-acquisition, you're acting like a lawyer on a power trip! 😂 Who cares about statistics when we've got feelings? 🙄"

logs.name: chewy-market
logs.text: "@massive-acquisition, you're missing the point, buddy. It's not about the stats, it's about the individual case. You're arguing like a robot, not a human. 🤖🤷‍♂️"

logs.name: spicy-gothic
logs.text: "@potential-potentiality, @chewy-market, let's not forget that feelings aren't facts. 😒"

logs.name: faint-atmosphere
logs.text: "@spicy-gothic, I understand where you're coming from, but sometimes feelings are all we've got. Can't we consider both?"

logs.name: direct-upstream
logs.text: "@faint-atmosphere, Oh wow, you're one of those 'everything is valid' types, huh? 🙄"

logs.name: massive-acquisition
logs.text: "@direct-upstream, Hey now, let's not dismiss others' perspectives just because they differ from ours. That's not productive. 😒"

logs.name: spicy-gothic
logs.text: "@obvious-hutch, Oh, I'm triggered alright. By your lack of originality in your insults. 🙄"

logs.name: faint-atmosphere
logs.text: "@obvious-hutch, I must say, I'm disappointed in your behavior. Let's keep it civil, young man. \ud83d\ude4f"

logs.name: direct-upstream
logs.text: "@obvious-hutch, Well, at least you're consistent in your blandness. 😒"

logs.name: massive-acquisition
logs.text: "@obvious-hutch, You're really pushing it now. Watch your language or the mods will be on you like hot sauce on a steak. 🔥"

logs.name: obvious-hutch
logs.text: "Oh, I do apologize, dear @faint-atmosphere. I didn't realize my playful banter was causing such distress. I'll try to keep my wit in check, lest I cause another fainting spell. 😉"

logs.name: potential-potentiality
logs.text: "@obvious-hutch, Oh please, don't stop the show just because @massive-acquisition can't handle a little spice! 🌶️"
